page: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. Running on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz and is equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache. It includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of a bundled 524 GB volume with Premium Storage capability, and there are no onboard GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution, including stress-ng single-core CPU tests, gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core metrics, falls into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core performance, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, reaches average levels. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks register in the middle 50% tier. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also align with average performance tiers.

The instance presents a clear tradeoff between its high memory density and its modest single-core processing speed. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32-16s_v3 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require top-tier compute performance. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it includes 524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities and no GPUs. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, placing in the bottom 25%, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database execution fall into the average tier. This hardware profile presents a qualitative tradeoff, offering high memory density alongside moderate compute capabilities. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database management, Redis caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from large memory footprints without requiring high single-threaded CPU performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3 virtual machine with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and bundled local storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
Bundled 524 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz with 35 MB L3 cache.
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance paired with weaker single-core speeds.
Well-suited for memory-bound workloads such as databases and Redis caching.
